title: Memories of a childhood in Tehran form a celebration of Iranian women | Aeon Videos
gist: A short blurb for Maryam Mohajer's animated film 'And Granny Would Dance', a child's-eye reflection on grief and a tribute to the women of Iran, drawn from the Iranian-British filmmaker's Tehran childhood memories.

insights.claim: Iranian-British filmmaker Maryam Mohajer's animated short 'And Granny Would Dance' draws on her Tehran childhood memories and shared stories to create a tribute to the women of Iran, told from a six-year-old's perspective.
insights.speaker: Aeon
insights.so_what: An instance of diaspora cultural production foregrounding Iranian women, a soft-power and cultural-memory signal from the Iranian-British creative community.
insights.quote: a heartfelt tribute to the women of Iran
insights.locator: Imbued with Mohajer’s knack for humour

insights.claim: The film treats grief through a child's eyes, centering a grandmother left melancholic and unable to smile since her husband's death silenced the accordion he played at home.
insights.speaker: Aeon
insights.so_what: Illustrates how animation is used to render grief and intergenerational memory, a recurring theme in the diaspora-memoir genre.
insights.quote: Granny, who has been melancholic since her husband passed away, silencing the tuneful accordion he would play at home, struggles to summon a smile
